# `breeze operator` groups each category by pipeline (sub-headers, sorted
# alphabetically) instead of one flat cross-pipeline list, and --pipeline/--env
# scope the whole surface (including --json) down to one pipeline/environment.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state

exec breeze identity register admin
cp stdout admin.token
exec breeze apply -f release.hcl --as admin --token-file admin.token
exec breeze apply -f other.hcl --as admin --token-file admin.token

! exec breeze stage start release build abc123 --as ci
! exec breeze stage start other build def456 --as ci

# Grouped by pipeline, alphabetically: "other" before "release".
exec breeze operator
stdout 'other:\n\s+build\s+def456'
stdout 'release:\n\s+build\s+abc123'

# --pipeline scopes the whole surface, including --json.
exec breeze operator --pipeline release
stdout 'release:'
! stdout 'other:'

exec breeze operator --pipeline release --json
stdout '"pipeline":\s*"release"'
! stdout '"pipeline":\s*"other"'

exec breeze stop

-- release.hcl --
pipeline "release" {
  stage "build" {
    type    = "command"
    timeout = "10s"
    command = ["/bin/false"]
  }
}

-- other.hcl --
pipeline "other" {
  stage "build" {
    type    = "command"
    timeout = "10s"
    command = ["/bin/false"]
  }
}
